title: Epstein-Barr virus-driven immunosuppression in nasopharyngeal carcinoma: a comprehensive review of viral mechanisms, spatial tumor ecosystems, and precision therapeutics.
abstract: EBV infection is the defining etiological factor in nasopharyngeal carcinoma (NPC), yet how viral factors systematically remodel the tumor immune microenvironment (TME) to sustain immunosuppression remains incompletely characterized. Existing reviews lack an integrated synthesis of viral mechanisms, TME spatial architecture, and therapeutic translation.
